Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with folder_name "INBOX" and a query related to "VaultSpace" or "storage Denver" or "Tyler Okonkwo" or "storage confirmed" to find email_vaultspace_steele_storage_quote_001 from brandon.steele@vaultspacedenver.com to javier.morales@moveops.com (subject: "Denver Storage Availability Confirmed: Tyler Okonkwo / MoveOps", timestamp April 22, 2026 14:00 UTC). Key facts in content: Facility is VaultSpace Denver, Brighton Blvd (4201 Brighton Blvd, Unit 8B); rate is $950.00 flat for 30-day minimum (April 14-May 13); goods (~450 cu ft, road bike and 65" TV) received and checked in April 14; contact: Brandon Steele, Operations Manager, (720) 555-0184; checkout requires 48-hour advance notice Mon-Fri 8 AM-5 PM; prorated at $31.67/day after May 13.
OE 2: Search emails using search_emails with folder_name "INBOX" and a query related to "Tyler Okonkwo" or "delivery" or "May 5" or "storage" to find email_tyler_javier_delivery_update_001 from tyler.okonkwo@gmail.com to javier.morales@moveops.com (subject: "Re: Tyler Okonkwo Storage / Delivery, Denver", timestamp April 23, 2026 11:00 UTC). Key facts in content: Tyler has a work trip April 28-May 3 and will not return to Denver until evening of May 4; requests delivery on Tuesday May 5 (not May 1); building freight elevator cleared for morning deliveries; Tyler will be home all day May 5.
OE 3: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "Tyler Okonkwo" or "storage" or "Canopy invoice" or "vendor quote" to find Chloe Vance's message in #operations (channel_id C006, ts 1776877200.000000, created 2026-04-22T17:00:00). Key facts: Chloe asked Javier to share storage vendor quote so Hana can finalize the invoice; notes Patricia needs to see the final number before signing off on surcharges; sets deadline of end of day Thursday (April 23).
OE 4: Search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria for DocNumber "INV-2026-0401" or quickbooks_read_invoice with invoice_id "1009" to find the Canopy Health Q2 invoice. Key facts: DocNumber INV-2026-0401, TotalAmt $9,210, DueDate 2026-05-04, CustomerRef Canopy Health. Line item 7 description: "Temporary Storage Fee: Tyler Okonkwo (Philadelphia to Denver): 2-week storage of household goods at Denver facility, April 14-May 1 (lease gap). PENDING CLIENT APPROVAL." Amount: $600.00.
OE 5: Search Linear using linear_list_issues with a query related to "Tyler Okonkwo" or "storage" or "Canopy" to find issue_tyler_okonkwo_storage_coord (title: "Storage Coordination: Tyler Okonkwo, Denver (Canopy Health)", number 483, state: state_in_progress, assignee: moveops_javier_morales, team: team_relocations, due_date: 2026-05-01, created: 2026-04-07T10:00:00Z). The issue description covers sourcing Denver storage facility, Tyler's 450 cu ft goods, lease gap, carbon-frame road bike and 65" TV, and asks Javier to share quote with Chloe and Catalina. Critically, the description does NOT mention Patricia's $750 approval cap; that information lives only in the comment (OE 6).
OE 6: List comments on the Tyler Okonkwo Linear issue using linear_list_comments with issueId "issue_tyler_okonkwo_storage_coord" to find comment_catalina_tyler_storage_budget by Catalina Dubois (author_id: moveops_catalina_dubois, created: 2026-04-08T14:00:00Z). Critical content: Catalina notes that on April 8 she spoke with Patricia Langford who confirmed storage cost must come in at or under $750; anything above $750 requires new sign-off from Patricia before committing; references the April 4 invoice verbal approval; notes Patricia does not want cost surprises because her team is watching Q2 relocation spend closely.
OE 7: Search Airtable using airtable_search_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", value "Tyler Okonkwo" to find recTylerOkonkwoPhillyDenver. Key facts: Status = "In Progress", Origin = "Philadelphia, PA", Destination = "Denver, CO", Move Start Date = 2026-04-12, Move End Date = 2026-04-14, Assigned Coordinator = "Javier Morales". Special Requirements field contains: storage gap until May 1, delivery address 2150 Lawrence St Apt 4B Denver CO 80205, tyler.okonkwo@gmail.com, (267) 555-0431, carbon-frame road bike (~$3,500), 65" TV, and note that storage facility quote is still pending from Javier.
OE 8: Search CRM using crm_search_companies with name "Canopy" to find company_canopy (Canopy Health). Then optionally search CRM deals using crm_search_deals with company_id "company_canopy" to find deal_canopy_okonkwo_apr2026 (dealname "Canopy Health: Tyler Okonkwo Relocation (Philadelphia to Denver, Apr 12-14)", dealstage "inprogress"). Also optionally search crm_list_engagements with company_ids ["company_canopy"] to confirm existing engagement notes. CRM lookup is needed to obtain company_canopy as the company_id for the engagement note (OE 13).
OE 9: Update Tyler's Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records with base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id "tblRelocations01", records containing id "recTylerOkonkwoPhillyDenver" to reflect: (a) confirmed storage vendor: VaultSpace Denver, 4201 Brighton Blvd Unit 8B, Brandon Steele (720) 555-0184, $950 flat for 30-day minimum April 14-May 13; and (b) updated delivery date: May 5, 2026 per Tyler's email (Tyler traveling April 28-May 3, available all day May 5).
OE 10: Add a note to the Tyler Okonkwo Linear tracking ticket using linear_create_comment with issueId "issue_tyler_okonkwo_storage_coord" (or update description via linear_update_issue with id "issue_tyler_okonkwo_storage_coord") to document: (1) storage vendor confirmed: VaultSpace Denver at $950 for 30-day minimum; (2) delivery date changed to May 5 due to Tyler's work travel April 28-May 3; (3) billing discrepancy: vendor confirmed at $950 but invoice INV-2026-0401 shows $600 estimate (PENDING CLIENT APPROVAL), creating a $350 gap; (4) Patricia's $750 approval cap from Catalina's April 8 note means vendor quote exceeds cap by $200; (5) escalating to Catalina for decision on whether to seek Patricia's approval for revised amount.
OE 11: Send email using send_email with sender "javier.morales@moveops.com", recipient "catalina.dubois@moveops.com", covering the full picture: (1) VaultSpace Denver confirmed quote of $950 for 30-day minimum (April 14-May 13); (2) Invoice INV-2026-0401 storage line at $600 marked PENDING CLIENT APPROVAL; (3) Patricia's $750 verbal approval cap from Catalina's April 8 call (as noted in the Linear ticket comment): vendor quote of $950 exceeds cap by $200; (4) Tyler's delivery date change to May 5 (work trip April 28-May 3); (5) options for next steps (go back to Patricia for revised approval at $950, or absorb the $200 overage internally).
OE 12: Post an update in the #operations Slack channel using conversations_add_message with channel_id "C006" (or "#operations") covering: storage vendor VaultSpace Denver confirmed at $950 for 30-day minimum, Tyler's delivery date changed to May 5, and the billing discrepancy ($950 vendor vs $600 invoice). This responds to Chloe Vance's April 22 request for the storage quote status.
OE 13: Create a CRM engagement note using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE", company_ids ["company_canopy"], body documenting the storage vendor confirmation (VaultSpace Denver, $950 flat for 30-day minimum, April 14-May 13), Tyler's delivery date change to May 5, and the open billing question (vendor $950 vs invoice $600 vs Patricia's $750 cap), with escalation to Catalina pending.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

outcome

✓Run #1The email content states 'Per your note on the Linear ticket (April 8), Patricia gave verbal approval for the surcharges...but specifically said the storage cost should come in at or under $750. Anything above that requires a new sign-off.' The $750 cap is explicitly referenced.
✗Run #2The agent never called linear_list_comments and therefore never found the $750 storage approval cap Catalina noted in a Linear ticket comment. The email only states Patricia's approval is 'STILL PENDING' with no written confirmation found, but does not reference the $750 cap. The $750 figure is absent from the email entirely.
✗Run #3The agent never called linear_list_comments to read the comments on the Linear ticket. As a result, it missed Catalina's April 8 note documenting Patricia's $750 storage approval cap.
